resources.string: Add a playlist to start importing content.
Add a playlist to get started.
Set up a playlist in settings first.
Select a provider and channel on the left.
Press OK to start the preview.
Set up a playlist in settings first.
Try a different spelling, a shorter search term, or part of the title.
Select a provider and movie category.
Add movies to your favorites via the favorites action.
Select a provider and series category.
Add series to your favorites via the favorites action.
Timeshift not available for this channel.
Progress could not be automatically determined.
Resume playback from the last position.
Started movies, series and recently watched live-TV channels appear here.
Set up a playlist in settings first. Started movies, series and recently watched channels will appear here.
Set up a provider in settings first.
This provider has no imported live-TV categories.
Select a channel to view its EPG.
Set up a playlist in settings first.
This provider has no imported live-TV channels.
This category has no imported live-TV channels.
Set up a provider in settings first.
Started movies appear here as long as they are not completed.
This provider has no imported movie categories.
This category has no imported movies.
This provider has no imported series categories.
This category has no imported series.
No episodes imported for this season.
Enter PIN to unlock the protected area.
The Android-TV target is missing, disabled, or requires updated credentials.
Play movie or episode externally? External players do not return automatic progress.
Timeshift window active. Live edge reached.
Search appears once local media data is loaded.
Live-TV, movies, series and EPG are searched locally.
%1$s currently has no local EPG data.
HTTP is allowed but treated as insecure.
Attempts to open Vivicast on Android TV startup if the system allows it.
Requires a second back confirmation from the top navigation.
System default or fixed app language.
Refresh playlists and EPG in the background.
Retain local list order per view.
Applies app-wide to HTTP requests. Empty uses the app default.
Adjusts output to match playback when the device supports it.
Applies to movies and single episodes only. Live-TV and catch-up remain internal.
Enables time-shifted live TV if the channel and stream support it.
Saved but not operable when timeshift is disabled.
Selects storage for the active timeshift buffer.
Preferred audio track if offered by the stream.
Preferred subtitle track if offered by the stream.
Applies to series episodes in the internal player.
Sets when the auto-next panel appears.
Saved but not operable when auto-next is disabled.
Passes compatible multi-channel formats directly to the output device.
EPG source saved. URL remains hidden.
Select an EPG source or create a new one.
EPG source deleted. Programs and assignments have been removed.
Applies only to the automatic interval refresh.
Cleanup only removes EPG program data outside this window.
Sources, provider assignments and manual mappings are retained.
Starts only when background refresh is enabled.
Separate trigger in addition to the interval.
Schedules a refresh according to the existing refresh plan.
Set up a playlist first to edit manual EPG assignments.
Set up a playlist first to edit EPG assignments.
Select a provider on the left, then a channel for manual EPG mapping.
This provider has no imported live-TV channels yet.
Select a provider and channel first.
Assign a source to this provider in the EPG sources section first.
This EPG source, its programs and assignments will be removed. Providers are retained.
Create an EPG source to assign it to providers.
Change name, time offset and active status. Reset URL only if needed.
URL is stored securely and not saved in Room.
Priority is configurable per provider. Manual channel mapping remains a separate flow.
Set up a playlist first to assign EPG sources.
Refresh of all URL and Xtream playlists started.
Playlists are configured locally. Credentials are stored outside the database.
Playlist deleted. Associated data has been removed.
No playlist available yet. Nothing will be imported.
The name already exists locally. Saving is blocked.
Allowed: M3U URL or complete M3U content.
Maps a local channel to an external channel ID from a linked EPG source.
Manual mapping removed. Automatic mapping may apply on next import.
Provider, channel, EPG source, and external channel ID must all be selected.
Provider, channel, and EPG source must be selected.
Focus and warning contrast remain enforced.
Panel and overlay readability stays limited.
Four digits, masked input via the system keyboard.
Opens movies only after PIN approval.
Opens series only after PIN approval.
Hides protected adult content without approval.
Active unlocks stay in memory and are not backed up.
New PIN must be four digits.
Restore replaces backup scope after validation and confirmation.
SMB and Google Drive credentials are not included in the standard backup.
Enter backup passphrase. It is not saved and protects the included credentials.
Timestamp of the last successful export on this device.
Encrypted with backup passphrase and includes source credentials.
Restore requires the backup passphrase and replaces backup scope.
Removes locally cached media images. Provider data remains.
Deletes Live TV history, movies, series, and search history.
Only locally cached media images are deleted. Provider data, history, favorites, EPG, and credentials remain.
Deletes recently watched Live TV channels.
Live TV history will be deleted. Movies, series, search, provider data, and favorites remain.
Movie history and playback progress will be deleted. Other histories remain.
Deletes series and episode playback progress.
Series history and episode playback progress will be deleted. Other histories remain.
Search history will be deleted. Playback history, provider data, and favorites remain.
Deletes Live TV, movies, series, and search.
Live TV history, movie and series progress, and search history will be deleted. Provider data and favorites remain.
General support data may be copied. Log content is not displayed and not copied.
Writes only sanitized technical events to private app storage.
Limits internal diagnostic sessions to 1 to 7 days.
Remains visible, but cannot be changed when diagnostic logging is off.
Creates a ZIP with vivicast-diagnostics.log and diagnostics-metadata.json.
Copies only the visible non-private technical information.
Vivicast is a local Android TV app. App-specific license notices are displayed locally and do not trigger network actions.
Vivicast processes local provider, EPG, media, and settings data on the device. There is no account, no cloud sync, and no telemetry.
Third-party components are referenced exclusively locally in the app. No network actions are triggered from this view.
Name is required and must be unique.
Source type cannot be changed after saving.
HTTPS is preferred. HTTP is visibly treated as insecure.
Clipboard can contain an M3U URL or complete M3U content.
Server, username, and password are required for the connection test.
Type remains unchanged. Modified sources must be tested again.
At least one content type must be active.
This action cannot be undone. Associated channels, categories, favorites, history, playback progress, and EPG assignments will be deleted. EPG sources remain.
Add a new source from M3U URL, file, clipboard, or Xtream Codes.
File is saved. Selecting a new file replaces the current source.
File ready. Save will replace the current source.
File is import-only. No permanent copy is stored.
File ready. Test connection and save.
Credentials are stored securely. No custom User-Agent.
File and clipboard sources can only be updated by replacement. Automatic refresh is disabled.
Starts a manual refresh for active sources.
Controls automatic updates; manual refresh remains available.
Set new value or leave empty
